title: Valid credentials allow login and redirect to dashboard
preconditions: Backend running at http://localhost:8000, frontend at http://localhost:5173, valid user credentials exist in the system
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:5173/login
Enter a valid username/email
Enter a valid password
Click the Login button
Observe the URL and page content after submission
expected_result: User is redirected to the CRA dashboard at '/', the heading 'Customer Risk Assessment (CRA)' is visible, and a session token is stored in localStorage or sessionStorage

title: Invalid credentials show error message without crashing
preconditions: Backend running, frontend running, user is on the /login page
steps: Navigate to http://localhost:5173/login
Enter an invalid username/email
Enter an incorrect password
Click the Login button
Observe the response on the page
expected_result: An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid credentials'), the user remains on the /login page, the application does not crash, and no session token is stored

title: Unauthenticated user accessing protected route is redirected to /login
preconditions: Frontend running, no session token present in localStorage/sessionStorage
steps: Ensure localStorage and sessionStorage are cleared
Navigate directly to http://localhost:5173/ (dashboard)
Observe the resulting URL and page content
expected_result: User is automatically redirected to /login and the login form is displayed instead of the dashboard

title: Session token is stored client-side after successful login
preconditions: Frontend running, valid credentials available
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter valid credentials and submit
After redirect to dashboard, open browser developer tools
Inspect localStorage and sessionStorage for a session token
expected_result: A session token key/value pair is present in localStorage or sessionStorage after successful authentication

title: Session token is included in subsequent API requests
preconditions: User is logged in and session token is stored client-side
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Navigate to the dashboard
Trigger a GET /api/customers request (automatic on page load)
Inspect the outgoing HTTP request headers in network tools
Also perform a POST, PUT, and DELETE on a customer record and inspect headers
expected_result: All subsequent API requests include the session token in the Authorization header (e.g., 'Bearer <token>') or as a cookie

title: Logout clears session and redirects to /login
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session token stored client-side
steps: Log in with valid credentials and confirm dashboard is visible
Click the Logout button/link
Observe the URL and page content
Inspect localStorage and sessionStorage for any remaining session token
expected_result: User is redirected to /login, the session token is removed from localStorage/sessionStorage, and accessing a protected route again redirects back to /login

title: Session persists across page refresh
preconditions: User is logged in, session token stored in localStorage
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Confirm dashboard is displayed
Press F5 or reload the browser page
Observe the URL and page content after reload
expected_result: User remains on the dashboard after page refresh without being redirected to /login, confirming session persistence via localStorage

title: Accessing protected API endpoints without token returns 401
preconditions: Backend running, no valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/customers without any Authorization header
Send POST /api/customers without any Authorization header
Send PUT /api/customers/{id} without any Authorization header
Send DELETE /api/customers/{id} without any Authorization header
expected_result: Each request returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized with an appropriate error message

title: Accessing protected API endpoints with an invalid or expired token returns 401
preconditions: Backend running
steps: Send GET /api/customers with Authorization header containing a malformed token (e.g., 'Bearer invalidtoken123')
Send GET /api/customers with Authorization header containing an expired token
Observe the response status and body
expected_result: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized for both malformed and expired tokens

title: Empty username and password fields show validation error
preconditions: Frontend running, user is on /login page
steps: Navigate to /login
Leave the username field empty
Leave the password field empty
Click the Login button
expected_result: Validation error messages are shown for both fields (e.g., 'Username is required', 'Password is required'), no API call is made, and the app does not crash

title: Empty username with valid password shows validation error
preconditions: Frontend running, user is on /login page
steps: Navigate to /login
Leave the username field empty
Enter a valid password
Click the Login button
expected_result: A validation error is shown for the username field, login does not proceed

title: Valid username with empty password shows validation error
preconditions: Frontend running, user is on /login page
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter a valid username
Leave the password field empty
Click the Login button
expected_result: A validation error is shown for the password field, login does not proceed

title: Extremely long username and password inputs are handled gracefully
preconditions: Frontend running, backend running
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter a username string of 10,000 characters
Enter a password string of 10,000 characters
Click the Login button
Observe the UI response and backend logs
expected_result: The application handles the large input gracefully — either truncates it client-side, shows a validation message, or the API returns a 400/422 error without crashing or hanging

title: Username with SQL injection characters does not break the system
preconditions: Frontend running, backend running
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter "' OR '1'='1" as the username
Enter any password
Click the Login button
Observe whether unauthorized access is granted
expected_result: Login fails with an invalid credentials error; no unauthorized access is granted; the application and database remain stable

title: Username with special characters and Unicode is handled correctly
preconditions: Frontend running, backend running
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter a username containing special characters such as '<script>alert(1)</script>'
Enter any password
Click the Login button
Observe UI behavior and HTTP response
expected_result: The input is sanitized or rejected; no XSS occurs; the application returns an invalid credentials error and does not execute injected scripts

title: Whitespace-only credentials are treated as invalid
preconditions: Frontend running, backend running
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter only spaces in the username field
Enter only spaces in the password field
Click the Login button
expected_result: The application treats whitespace-only inputs as empty/invalid, shows a validation or credentials error, and does not proceed to the dashboard

title: Regression: existing CRA page load and listing still works after auth is added
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session token, frontend and backend running
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Navigate to '/'
Wait for the page to fully load
Check that the heading 'Customer Risk Assessment (CRA)' is visible
Check that 'All CRA Records' section is visible and the customer table loads
expected_result: The CRA dashboard loads correctly for authenticated users, exactly as it did before auth was introduced — no regressions in page structure or data display

title: Regression: GET /api/customers still returns customer list for authenticated requests
preconditions: Backend running, valid session token available
steps: Send GET /api/customers with a valid Authorization header
Inspect the response status and body
expected_result: HTTP 200 is returned with a JSON array of customer records matching the CustomerOut schema, unchanged from pre-auth behavior

title: Regression: Create customer (POST /api/customers) still calculates risk score for authenticated user
preconditions: User is logged in, backend running
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Fill in the New Customer KYC form with all required fields
Click Submit
Observe the response and newly created record in the table
expected_result: Customer is created successfully, risk score (0–100) and risk level (Low/Medium/High) are calculated and displayed in the table, same as before auth was added

title: Regression: Edit and update customer (PUT /api/customers/{id}) still recalculates risk for authenticated user
preconditions: User is logged in, at least one customer record exists
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Click Edit on an existing customer record
Modify one or more fields
Click Update Customer
Observe the updated record in the table
expected_result: Customer record is updated successfully, risk score and level are recalculated, and the updated values are reflected in the table

title: Regression: Delete customer (DELETE /api/customers/{id}) still works for authenticated user
preconditions: User is logged in, at least one customer record exists
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Click Delete on an existing customer record
Confirm deletion if a confirmation dialog appears
Observe the table after deletion
expected_result: The customer record is removed from the table and the backend returns a success response, same behavior as before auth was introduced

title: Regression: GET /api/risk-reference still accessible to authenticated users
preconditions: Backend running, valid session token available
steps: Send GET /api/risk-reference with a valid Authorization header
Inspect the response status and body
expected_result: HTTP 200 is returned with the risk reference dictionaries, unchanged from pre-auth behavior

title: Expired session token redirects user to /login on protected route access
preconditions: Frontend running, an expired session token is manually placed in localStorage
steps: Manually set an expired token in localStorage under the session token key
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/ (the dashboard)
Observe the URL and page content
expected_result: The frontend detects the expired token, removes it from storage, and redirects the user to /login

title: Tampered/corrupted session token in localStorage is rejected
preconditions: Frontend running, backend running
steps: Log in and obtain a valid session token
Manually alter a few characters of the stored token in localStorage
Reload the page or navigate to a protected route
Observe redirect behavior on UI and API response if a request is made
expected_result: The corrupted token is rejected; the user is redirected to /login on the UI; any API request with the tampered token returns HTTP 401

title: Navigating to /login while already authenticated redirects to dashboard
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session token stored client-side
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Manually navigate to http://localhost:5173/login
Observe the resulting URL and page content
expected_result: Already-authenticated user is automatically redirected away from /login to the dashboard, preventing duplicate login

title: Login API endpoint returns token on valid credentials
preconditions: Backend running, valid user credentials exist
steps: Send a POST request to the login endpoint (e.g., /api/auth/login or /api/token) with valid credentials in the request body
Inspect the response status and body
expected_result: HTTP 200 is returned with a response body containing a session/access token field

title: Login API endpoint returns 401 on invalid credentials
preconditions: Backend running
steps: Send a POST request to the login endpoint with an incorrect username
Send a POST request to the login endpoint with a correct username but wrong password
Observe both responses
expected_result: Both requests return HTTP 401 Unauthorized with an error message; no token is issued

title: Login API endpoint returns 422 on missing required fields
preconditions: Backend running
steps: Send a POST request to the login endpoint with an empty JSON body '{}'
Send a POST request with only the username field and no password
Observe the response status and validation error details
expected_result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned with validation error details for missing required fields

title: Multiple failed login attempts do not lock out or crash the system
preconditions: Backend running, frontend running
steps: Navigate to /login
Submit invalid credentials 10 consecutive times
Observe the UI behavior and API responses throughout
expected_result: Each attempt returns the invalid credentials error; the application remains stable; the UI does not freeze or crash; optionally, rate limiting or lockout behavior is observed after a threshold

title: Case sensitivity of username/email during login
preconditions: Backend running, a user with a known username/email exists
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter the username in uppercase (e.g., 'ADMIN' instead of 'admin')
Enter the correct password
Click Login and observe the result
expected_result: Login behavior is consistent with the system's case sensitivity policy — either succeeds if case-insensitive or returns an error if case-sensitive; behavior is documented and predictable
